rationale: Standard single-module Go CLI with go.mod at root and conventional package layout; native Gazelle Go generation over the whole tree is the obvious path. Tests rely on testdata fixtures which Gazelle bundles as data deps.
module_bazel:
module(
    name = "gitleaks",
    version = "0.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_go", version = "0.61.1")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.51.3")

go_sdk = use_extension("@rules_go//go:extensions.bzl", "go_sdk")
go_sdk.download(version = "1.25.4")

go_deps = use_extension("@gazelle//:extensions.bzl", "go_deps")
go_deps.from_file(go_mod = "//:go.mod")
root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle")

# gazelle:prefix github.com/zricethezav/gitleaks/v8
# gazelle:go_naming_convention import_alias
gazelle(name = "gazelle")
anticipated_risks: go:embed of config/gitleaks.toml and report_templates/*.tmpl needs embedsrcs which Gazelle handles but data globs may miss some testdata
tests read testdata/ fixtures (incl. dotGit dirs) via relative paths — package data deps may need broader globs
wasilibs_regex.go may use cgo or wasm build tags / external WASM module
regexp package has build-tag-gated alternative files (stdlib_regex.go vs wasilibs_regex.go)
cmd/generate/secrets/regen.go and config tmpl codegen could expect runtime file access
